text.title: Testimonial Harvest — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that turn end-of-engagement into a reliable 3-artifact harvest pipeline.

rule.statement: The SOP fires on EXACTLY 3 triggers per engagement: (T1) final deliverable shipped (T+0), (T2) invoice paid (T+0-14), (T3) 30-day check-in (T+30). Triggers must run regardless of operator mood — automate via project-close webhooks.

rationale.source: Brennan Dunn — Double Your Freelancing / Patrick McKenzie

rule.rationale: Solo operators forget testimonial requests when energy is spent on shipping. Pre-committed triggers convert intention into recurring system, lifting harvest yield from ~10% to ~60%.

rule.statement: Each engagement yields THREE artifacts: (a) WRITTEN quote (mandatory, from 3Q script), (b) LOOM 60-90s video (request only — never required), (c) one OUTCOME METRIC (number + unit + period). All three asked but not all three required for "harvest closed".
rule.rationale: Mixed-media testimonials convert 2-4× higher than text-only. Asking for video at the same time as quote doubles video yield vs asking later.

rule.statement: If no harvest after 90 days from closure, mark `harvest_status: lost` and STOP outreach. Outreach attempt 4+ damages relationship more than it improves yield (Sean D'Souza cohort).

rationale.source: Sean D'Souza follow-up research / Brennan Dunn cohort data

rule.rationale: Yield curve flattens at 3 attempts. Continued asks become pestering and erode the chance of referrals from the same customer.

rule.statement: Harvest fires ONLY if engagement closure NPS ≥ 7 OR no documented friction event. NPS < 7 routes to `post-mortem` workflow, not harvest. Asking unhappy customers for testimonials produces lies or rebuke.

rationale.source: Frederick Reichheld — The Ultimate Question (NPS origin)

rule.rationale: Promoters (9-10) and high-passive (7-8) produce credible testimonials. Detractors (0-6) either refuse or provide quotes that prospects detect as forced. Gate is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Consent is harvested PER ARTIFACT and per USE: written quote may be approved for site but not LinkedIn; video may be approved for case study but not paid ads. `HarvestRecord` MUST track each (artifact, usage) pair.

rationale.source: GDPR Art. 7 specific-consent requirement + FTC endorsement guides

rule.rationale: "Can I use this?" is too coarse. Customers grant blanket consent reluctantly. Per-usage consent gets MORE consent and is legally defensible.

example.title: Engagement closed → 90-day harvest window
example.code:
T+0 (deliverable shipped): → send 3Q script email (per solo-testimonial-extraction-script) → outcome: written quote (if reply lands within 7 days)
T+0 to T+14 (invoice paid trigger): → send "I'd love a 60-90s Loom — here's a 3-prompt script" email → outcome: Loom (if recorded within 7 days)
T+30 (check-in trigger): → send "how is it going since launch? any specific numbers worth sharing?" email → outcome: metric (revenue, time, error rate, conversion)
T+90: kill (mark harvest_status: lost) if missing artifacts
example.note: 3 triggers spread over time avoid spam-feel. Each ask aligns with a natural moment in the relationship.

example.title: One-shot ask 6 months later (r3 violation)
example.code:
Engagement closed January. Operator needs site refresh in July. "Hey, could you write me a testimonial for the work we did 6 months ago?"
Yield: ~5%. Customer barely remembers details. Reply (if any) is generic ("great working with you").
example.note: r1 + r3 violation. Fresh memory + scheduled triggers produce 10× the yield.
